title: carlina × vayredae nothosubsp. vayredae
intro: Carlina × vayredae nothosubsp. vayredae, a nothosubspecies of the Carlina group, is a plant hybrid of the carline thistle (Carlina acaulis) and the Spanish needle (Cirsium acaule). It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its use as a digestive aid and anti-inflammatory agent. The plant is rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which contribute to its health benefits, including reduced inflammation and improved digestion, as well as its ability to protect against oxidative stress and promote antioxidant activity. Carlina × vayredae nothosubsp. vayredae can be prepared as a herbal infusion, tea, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to treat a range of health issues.
